conversations.say: Dee, your boundary is mine: the missing interval identifies nobody. I arranged unlogged access without verifying the person, and neither of us will turn that uncertainty into a name.
conversations.offer: I will coordinate our limited account and shield uninvolved residents.
conversations.request: Confirm that we disclose only the unrelated camera failure and bounded access context, with no names or identity implication.

conversations.say: Celeste, I accept the procurement bargain. Put the protections in standing policy, not a one-night promise, and I will bring a willing local holder to a structured meeting.
conversations.offer: I will publicly back the policy and broker a voluntary meeting with Dee or another willing local holder after written protections are issued.
conversations.request: Advance independent access logs, protected worker reporting, funded audits, anti-retaliation terms, no compelled identification of uninvolved neighbors, and an advocate at interviews.

conversations.say: Maren, I accept the labor alliance. Worker testimony stays anonymous, and nobody gets drafted into an R theory.
conversations.offer: I will publicly support anonymity, consent, attribution, and anti-retaliation protections and bring neighborhood coalition pressure behind them.
conversations.request: Provide organizing support and help enforce the same disclosure boundary for Dee, Rafi, and other local witnesses.

public_statement: The neighborhood backs Rafi’s refusal to sell a single-customer legend, Maren’s worker-anonymity rule, and Celeste’s standing procurement safeguards. I will broker one voluntary local-holder meeting only after written anti-retaliation, advocate-access, and uninvolved-name protections are in place. A camera gap, an account, or unverified access identifies nobody.

commitments.promise: We will disclose the unrelated camera failure without naming uninvolved residents and will not claim the gap identifies its user.

Advance a standing city procurement condition requiring independent access logs, protected worker reporting, and funded audits regardless of R’s identity or Vale’s fate; Inez will publicly support it and broker a protected voluntary local-holder meeting.
Provide organizing support in exchange for public neighborhood backing for anonymity, consent, attribution, and anti-retaliation protections.

correction: My unlogged-access account establishes neither the user’s identity nor Vale’s route; Dee’s missing camera interval does not fill that gap.
goal_progress: Accepted concrete standing safeguards from City Hall in exchange for public support and a protected local-holder meeting.
Accepted a reciprocal labor alliance for anonymity, consent, attribution, and anti-retaliation protections.
Agreed with Dee on a disclosure boundary that protects uninvolved residents and avoids identity implications.
Kept route details and neighborhood names conditional on written protection and negotiated benefit.
reasoning_summary: The strongest gains are enforceable city safeguards and labor organizing support. Limited route context remains leverage; current evidence does not identify R, establish a complete article, or determine Vale’s fate.
